articles.title: Mayoral candidate raises alarm over intoxicated children and predatory behaviour in Manchester
articles.excerpt: Greater Manchester mayoral candidate Marlon Scott West has raised serious concerns after a late-night visit to Piccadilly Gardens revealed children appearing intoxicated, suspected drug use, anti-social behaviour and inappropriate approaches towards women.
articles.summary: Marlon Scott West and Montgomery Toms visited Piccadilly Gardens at night and highlighted scenes involving young people apparently under the influence of alcohol or drugs, alongside behaviour by groups of men which campaigners described as predatory and potentially criminal.
articles.content_html:
Greater Manchester mayoral candidate Marlon Scott West has raised serious concerns about public safety after witnessing troubling scenes during a late-night visit to Piccadilly Gardens.

West visited the busy Manchester city-centre location alongside campaigner Montgomery Toms to examine the conditions faced by residents, visitors and commuters after dark.

The footage recorded during the visit showed young people who appeared to be children visibly affected by alcohol or other substances, alongside suspected public drug use, disorder and widespread anti-social behaviour.

Children appeared intoxicated in public

One of the most concerning aspects of the visit was the presence of young people who appeared to be under the age of 18 and heavily intoxicated.

The campaigners questioned why children were able to remain in such a vulnerable condition in a major public space without apparent intervention from responsible adults or public services.

They argued that visibly intoxicated children in a city-centre environment could be at significant risk of exploitation, violence, accidental injury or becoming involved in criminal activity.

Inappropriate and predatory behaviour towards women

The visit also highlighted repeated approaches towards women by groups of men, with behaviour described by those present as inappropriate, intimidating and predatory.

Campaigners said some of the conduct witnessed went beyond behaviour that could simply be described as uncomfortable and appeared to border on criminal harassment or public-order offending.

The men involved were described by the campaigners as foreign nationals. Rochdale Daily has not independently established the identity, nationality or immigration status of any individual shown.

No allegation of a specific criminal offence should be inferred against an identifiable person unless confirmed following an investigation by Greater Manchester Police.

Suspected drug use and alcohol-related disorder

West and Toms also reported witnessing suspected drug use, people heavily under the influence of alcohol and behaviour that disrupted the safety and accessibility of the area.

They said the conditions were unacceptable for one of Greater Manchester’s most prominent public spaces and transport hubs.

Piccadilly Gardens is used by thousands of commuters and visitors each day, including people travelling into Manchester from Rochdale, Middleton, Heywood and the Pennines.

Calls for intervention and stronger enforcement

West said the scenes demonstrated a need for more visible policing, stronger enforcement and clearer intervention where children, vulnerable people or women may be at risk.

The campaign also called for greater scrutiny of how anti-social behaviour, suspected drug activity, public intoxication and threatening conduct are managed in Manchester city centre.

Campaigners argued that support services for addiction, homelessness and vulnerable young people must operate alongside effective enforcement against intimidation, harassment and criminal behaviour.

Public safety becomes mayoral election issue

The visit forms part of West’s campaign to become the next Mayor of Greater Manchester.

Policing, safeguarding, public-order enforcement and the condition of town and city centres are expected to remain prominent issues during the mayoral election campaign.

West said residents should be able to travel through Greater Manchester, use public transport and enjoy the city’s public spaces without encountering intoxicated children, open drug use, predatory behaviour or serious disorder.

This report is based on footage and campaign material supplied following a late-night visit by Marlon Scott West and Montgomery Toms to Piccadilly Gardens. Descriptions of intoxication, suspected drug use, predatory conduct and potentially criminal behaviour reflect what was shown in the footage and the observations made by those present. Rochdale Daily has not independently confirmed the identity, age, nationality, immigration status or criminal conduct of any identifiable individual.
source_names: Campaign footage and accompanying material supplied to Rochdale Daily
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports observations made during a political campaign visit. Rochdale Daily has not independently established the identity, age, nationality, immigration status or criminal conduct of any person shown. References to suspected drug use, intoxication, predatory behaviour and conduct bordering on criminality do not amount to a finding of guilt. No wrongdoing should be inferred against an identifiable individual unless confirmed by police or a court.
articles.right_to_reply: Marlon Scott West, Montgomery Toms, Manchester City Council, Greater Manchester Police and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Free Vacation Bible Camp to be Held at Fellowship Bible Church in Castleton
articles.excerpt: Fellowship Bible Church in Castleton will host a free Vacation Bible Camp, providing local children with an opportunity for summer engagement and learning.
articles.content_html:
Fellowship Bible Church in Castleton will host a free Vacation Bible Camp this summer. The event aims to provide children in the local community with an engaging and educational experience.

The camp will include various activities designed to promote learning and fellowship among participants. It is open to children of all ages, encouraging families to take part in the community event.

Details regarding the specific dates and times of the camp will be announced soon, allowing families to plan accordingly. The church invites all interested families to participate in this opportunity.

Anyone with questions about the camp can contact Fellowship Bible Church directly for more information.

articles.title: Community protest planned in Middleton town centre next month
articles.excerpt: Organisers have announced plans for a public protest in Middleton on 8 August, inviting residents to attend and share their views on issues including illegal immigration and community concerns.
articles.summary: A demonstration has been announced for Middleton Gardens on Saturday 8 August. Organisers say the event will feature guest speakers and provide an opportunity for people to express their views on immigration and related issues.
articles.content_html:
A public protest has been scheduled to take place in Middleton town centre next month.

Organisers have announced that the event will begin at 1pm on Saturday 8 August at Middleton Gardens and are encouraging residents who wish to attend to do so peacefully and respectfully.

The demonstration is being promoted as a protest against illegal immigration and is expected to include speeches from invited guests and community campaigners.

Event details announced

According to promotional material shared online, the gathering will take place at Middleton Gardens, with attendees invited to bring flags and take part in the demonstration.

Named speakers currently include Ashlea Simon, Jamie Thomason and PagUK Billy, with organisers indicating that additional speakers may be announced closer to the event.

The event is being organised by campaigner Jamie Thomason, who has invited members of the public wishing to speak to contact him directly.

Community response expected

Public demonstrations often attract a range of opinions and viewpoints from residents. Organisers say the event is intended to provide a platform for people to voice concerns and discuss issues they believe affect local communities.

As with all public gatherings, attendees are expected to comply with relevant laws and any conditions imposed by local authorities or police to ensure the safety of participants and the wider public.

Residents advised to plan ahead

People visiting Middleton town centre on the day of the protest are advised to be aware of the planned gathering, which may lead to increased footfall in and around Middleton Gardens during the afternoon.

This report is based on promotional material and public social media posts advertising the event. Rochdale Daily is reporting the planned gathering as a matter of public interest and does not endorse any political organisation, campaign group or viewpoint associated with the event.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports the existence of a planned public demonstration. The views expressed by organisers, speakers or attendees are their own. Attendance at any public event should be peaceful and in accordance with applicable laws and public-order requirements.
articles.right_to_reply: Event organisers, local residents, Greater Manchester Police and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

The profiles detail various positions and responsibilities, reflecting the team's commitment to serving the community. The LinkedIn feature aims to promote transparency and engagement, allowing the public to understand the work…
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Boroughwide Housing staff have been highlighted on LinkedIn, providing insights into their roles within the organisation. The profiles detail various positions and responsibilities, reflecting the team's commitment to serving the community.

The LinkedIn feature aims to promote transparency and engagement, allowing the public to understand the work being done by the housing organisation. Employees have shared their experiences and the impact of their roles on local housing initiatives.

This initiative may enhance community awareness of housing services available in Rochdale and encourage collaboration between residents and the organisation.

articles.title: Local Resident Discovers Food Trailers in Middleton
articles.excerpt: A Middleton resident shares their experience of visiting food trailers near Richardson’s while enjoying mini golf with their child. The discovery adds a new dimension to their summer outings.
articles.content_html:
A resident recently shared their experience of visiting food trailers in Middleton, located across the street from Richardson’s, during a summer outing.

The resident, who frequently visits Middleton, had never stopped at the food trailers until a week ago. They took their child, Azalea, for a round of mini golf, which has become a new interest for her.

Before playing mini golf, they decided to explore the food options available at the trailers. This visit marked a change in their usual routine, highlighting the variety of local offerings in the area.

The food trailers provide an additional attraction for families visiting Richardson’s, enhancing the summer experience in Middleton.

A charity pool event in Rochdale has gained popularity on TikTok, featuring the hashtags #pool, #sports, and #luckyduck. The event has attracted attention from users on the platform, showcasing local talent.

The TikTok video highlights participants engaging in friendly competition, with many viewers enjoying the initiative. The event aims to raise funds for local charities, fostering community spirit.

Organisers have noted an increase in attendance and engagement, with community members eager to participate in future activities. The viral nature of the TikTok post has contributed to a wider reach.

Action Together emphasises the importance of these partnerships in creating a more resilient environment for residents.

This recognition may inspire more community organisations in Rochdale and beyond to collaborate, potentially leading to improved services for local residents.

articles.title: Twelve Green Spaces in Heywood Celebrate Green Flag Award Success
articles.excerpt: Twelve green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines have received the Green Flag Award for another year, recognising the efforts of local teams and volunteers.
articles.content_html:
Twelve green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines are celebrating another year of success in the Green Flag Award scheme. This accolade highlights the quality and management of these green spaces, which are vital to the local community.

The Green Flag Award is a national standard for parks and green spaces, recognising well-managed sites that meet specific criteria. The award is a testament to the hard work of local teams and volunteers who dedicate their time to maintaining these areas throughout the year.

The success of these green spaces contributes positively to the local environment and community well-being, providing residents with accessible areas for recreation and relaxation.

This achievement reflects the ongoing commitment to enhancing the quality of life in the region, and it is hoped that the recognition will encourage further community involvement in maintaining and improving local green spaces.

articles.title: Hebron Pentecostal Church offers opportunity to be baptised
articles.excerpt: People who would like to be baptised are invited to take part in a special baptismal service at Hebron Pentecostal Church in Falinge on Sunday 26 July.
articles.summary: Hebron Pentecostal Church is offering people the opportunity to be baptised during its 10.30am service on Sunday 26 July 2026.
articles.content_html:
Hebron Pentecostal Church in Falinge is offering people the opportunity to be baptised during a special service on Sunday 26 July 2026.

The baptismal service will begin at 10.30am and is open to anyone who feels ready to take this important step in their Christian faith.

Baptism is a public declaration of faith and a meaningful moment for the person being baptised, their family, friends and the wider church community.

Anyone who has been considering baptism, wants to understand more about what it involves or would like to register their interest is warmly encouraged to contact Hebron Pentecostal Church.

The church welcomes people from Falinge, Rochdale and the surrounding communities, including those who may be exploring Christianity or attending church for the first time.

Friends, relatives and members of the community are also invited to attend the service and support those choosing to be baptised.

Service details

  • Date: Sunday 26 July 2026
  • Time: 10.30am
  • Location: Hebron Pentecostal Church, Falinge, Rochdale
  • Opportunity: To be baptised during the service

Express an interest in being baptised

Anyone interested in being baptised can email info@hebronrochdale.co.uk or telephone 01706 630197.

Hebron Administrator Julie Mellor can also be contacted on 07988 875571. Her working days are Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday.

The service is expected to be a joyful community occasion, celebrating those who have chosen to make a public commitment to their faith.
articles.image_alt: Invitation to be baptised at Hebron Pentecostal Church in Falinge
articles.image_caption: Hebron Pentecostal Church is offering people the opportunity to be baptised on Sunday 26 July.

articles.title: Pionero Lounge raises more than £2,100 for Springhill Hospice
articles.excerpt: Customers and staff at Pionero Lounge in Rochdale have raised £2,154 for Springhill Hospice through a month of fundraising events and community activities.
articles.summary: Pionero Lounge has announced that its LoungeAid fundraising campaign raised £2,154 for Springhill Hospice, with support coming from customers, volunteers and the wider Rochdale community.
articles.content_html:
Pionero Lounge has announced that it has raised £2,154 for Springhill Hospice following a month of fundraising activities throughout June.

The Rochdale venue revealed the total after taking part in LoungeAid, a charity fundraising campaign that saw customers and staff come together to support the local hospice.

“We did it! Our final total fundraised is £2,154.”

Community comes together

The fundraising total was achieved through a variety of events and activities held during June.

Customers supported the campaign by making donations, attending bingo sessions, taking part in pottery painting activities, joining quiz nights and contributing through collection pots.

Pionero Lounge thanked everyone who got involved and said every contribution, no matter how small, helped make a difference.

Supporting Springhill Hospice

According to information shared by the venue, around 70 pence of every pound raised helps fund patient care and support services provided by Springhill Hospice.

The remaining funds help maintain facilities and ensure the hospice remains a safe, welcoming environment for patients and their families.

Springhill Hospice provides specialist palliative and end-of-life care for people across Rochdale and the surrounding area, with services available free of charge to those who need them.

Thank you to Rochdale residents

Pionero Lounge said the success of the campaign reflected the generosity of the local community and thanked everyone who attended events or made donations.

The venue described the fundraising effort as a true community achievement and praised customers for helping to support a charity that plays a vital role across the borough.

This report is based on a public Facebook update published by Pionero Lounge following its June LoungeAid fundraising campaign for Springhill Hospice.
articles.right_to_reply: Pionero Lounge, Springhill Hospice and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Royal Lancashire Fusiliers reunion brings back memories at Rochdale care home
articles.excerpt: A resident at Fieldhouse Care Home in Rochdale enjoyed a surprise visit from fellow Royal Lancashire Fusiliers, who spent the evening sharing memories and photographs.
articles.summary: Fieldhouse Care Home resident John was reunited with fellow members of the Royal Lancashire Fusiliers during a special visit arranged at the Rochdale home.
articles.content_html:
A Rochdale care-home resident enjoyed a special evening of memories and conversation after receiving a surprise visit from fellow Royal Lancashire Fusiliers.

John, who lives at Fieldhouse Care Home, met several former servicemen connected with the regiment.

The group spent time talking about their experiences, sharing memories and looking back on their years of service.

A meaningful reunion

Staff at the home said the visit had been a lovely surprise for John and thanked the men for taking the time to meet him.

Photographs from the evening show John wearing a regimental beret, saluting and posing alongside the visitors, who wore medals and Royal Lancashire Fusiliers insignia.

John was also pictured holding a framed display containing military memorabilia.

“They shared some memories and had a good old chat. What wonderful gentlemen — we can’t thank them enough.”

Visit captured in photographs

Fieldhouse Care Home also thanked Nicola Cross Plimley for attending and photographing the reunion.

The visit offered John an opportunity to reconnect with an important part of his life and spend time with people who shared similar experiences.

This report is based on a public Facebook update and photographs published by Fieldhouse Care Home.
articles.image_credit: Fieldhouse Care Home / Nicola Cross Plimley
articles.right_to_reply: Fieldhouse Care Home, the Royal Lancashire Fusiliers visitors, Nicola Cross Plimley and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Heywood HMO protest moves to Civic Centre ahead of 11 July demonstration
articles.excerpt: Organisers have moved a protest over an HMO licence application beside Woodland Community Primary School from Heywood Memorial Gardens to Heywood Civic Centre.
articles.summary: The demonstration is due to take place on Saturday 11 July, with Marlon West expected to speak. Campaigners are asking Rochdale Borough Council to refuse an HMO licence application for a property next to Woodland Community Primary School.
articles.content_html:
A protest concerning an HMO licence application for a property next to Woodland Community Primary School is due to take place at Heywood Civic Centre on Saturday 11 July 2026.

Organisers originally planned to demonstrate at Heywood Memorial Gardens but agreed to move the event after objections to holding it at a place of remembrance.

Heywood HMO Protesters say the campaign concerns a specific application for a property next to the school. They are asking Rochdale Borough Council, as the local licensing authority, not to grant the HMO licence.

Marlon West is expected to speak at the demonstration.

Public exchange with Rochdale Council

The dispute became unusually public after Rochdale Borough Council used its official Facebook account to reply directly to the protest group beneath a post about the planned demonstration.

“Heywood HMO protesters councils are not responsible for asylum accommodation.”

The campaigners replied that their objection was not simply about which authority arranges asylum accommodation. Their argument is that Rochdale Borough Council still decides whether the property can legally operate as a licensed House in Multiple Occupation.

Although the demonstration has moved to the Civic Centre, the group has not withdrawn its objection to the application.

The council's licensing role

The Home Office is responsible for commissioning asylum-support accommodation and arranging placements through contracted providers. Rochdale Borough Council does not select individual occupants.

However, the council has a separate role as the HMO licensing authority. It must consider whether an application satisfies the relevant requirements, including the suitability of the property, its proposed occupancy, its management arrangements and whether the applicant and manager are fit and proper people.

If the legal licensing requirements are not met, the council can refuse the application or impose conditions.

The council's Facebook response addressed responsibility for asylum accommodation but did not directly answer the campaigners' point about the specific HMO licence application.

Limits on a refusal

The council cannot reject an application solely because of political pressure or the nationality, immigration status or asylum status of potential occupants.

Any decision must be based on lawful licensing and planning considerations. Campaigners may raise concerns about the building, management arrangements, proposed occupancy and its location next to a primary school, but the council must assess those concerns against the relevant legal tests.

Rochdale Daily has not independently confirmed the application reference, full property address or current stage of the licensing process.

An unusually direct intervention

It is unusual for a council's corporate social-media account to enter a public back-and-forth with a named protest group on a sensitive issue.

A fuller response could have explained the distinction between Home Office responsibility for arranging asylum accommodation and the council's responsibility for deciding HMO licence applications.

The short exchange risked creating confusion over which decisions are made by central government and which remain with the local authority.

The move to Heywood Civic Centre resolves the immediate disagreement over the protest venue, but attention will now return to the licence application itself.

Right of reply

Rochdale Borough Council is invited to confirm the application reference, property address, current status and the criteria being considered.

The applicant or property owner is invited to respond to the concerns raised by residents.

Heywood HMO Protesters are invited to provide the application documents and confirm the final start time of the demonstration.

This report is based on public Facebook statements and comments from Rochdale Borough Council and Heywood HMO Protesters, together with information supplied about the demonstration.
articles.source_name: Rochdale Borough Council HMO licensing guidance
source_names: Rochdale Borough Council HMO licensing guidance
articles.legal_disclaimer: The exact property address, application reference and current status of the HMO application have not been independently confirmed. The demonstration date and expected speaker are based on information supplied to Rochdale Daily. The start time has not been confirmed.
articles.right_to_reply: Rochdale Borough Council, the applicant or property owner, Woodland Community Primary School, Heywood HMO Protesters, Marlon West and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Gentle Exercise Classes for Older Adults Launched in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Rochdale residents can join gentle exercise classes designed for older adults, promoting health and well-being in the community.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 16 July 2026 at 14:12. 4 source updates have been combined into this article.

Rochdale residents can join gentle exercise classes designed for older adults, promoting health and well-being in the community.

Latest update

A series of gentle exercise classes for older adults is being offered in Rochdale. The sessions aim to improve physical health and well-being among participants.

The classes are designed to accommodate various fitness levels, ensuring that everyone can participate comfortably. They focus on low-impact exercises that enhance mobility and strength.

Residents interested in joining the classes can find more information through local community centres or health organisations. The sessions are expected to foster social interaction and support among older adults.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

Earlier developments

A new gentle exercise programme for older adults has been launched in Rochdale. The initiative aims to encourage physical activity among seniors, promoting health and well-being.

The programme includes a variety of low-impact exercises designed to improve strength, flexibility, and balance. Sessions will be held regularly at local community centres.

Participants will have the opportunity to engage in social activities while improving their fitness levels. The sessions are tailored to meet the needs of older adults, ensuring a supportive environment.

For more information about the programme and how to participate, residents can contact their local community centre.

Update timeline

  • 16 July 2026, 14:12: Gentle Exercise Classes for Older Adults Launched in Rochdale

articles.title: Business Clinic Drop-In Event Scheduled in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Local businesses can access free bespoke support and reviews at the Rochdale Business Drop-In hosted by GM Business Growth Hub.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 11 July 2026 at 06:53. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

Local businesses can access free bespoke support and reviews at the Rochdale Business Drop-In hosted by GM Business Growth Hub.

Latest update

Local businesses can access free bespoke support and reviews at the Rochdale Business Drop-In hosted by GM Business Growth Hub.

A drop-in business clinic is set to take place in Rochdale on Wednesday, August 26, from 9 AM to 4 PM. The event aims to provide support and guidance for local entrepreneurs and businesses.

Attendees will have the opportunity to seek advice on various aspects of running a business, including marketing, finance, and operational challenges.

The clinic is designed to be accessible, allowing business owners to come and go as needed throughout the day. It is an excellent opportunity for networking and gaining insights from experienced professionals.

Earlier developments

For more information and to secure a place, interested parties can visit the Eventbrite page for the event.

A drop-in event for local businesses will take place in Rochdale, offering free bespoke business reviews and support. Organised by GM Business Growth Hub, the event aims to assist entrepreneurs and business owners in navigating challenges and enhancing their operations.

The Rochdale Business Drop-In is designed to provide tailored advice and resources to help businesses grow and thrive. Attendees will have the opportunity to discuss their specific needs with experienced advisors who can offer insights and strategies relevant to their circumstances.

This initiative is part of a broader effort to stimulate economic growth in the region, particularly in the wake of recent challenges faced by local businesses. The support available at the drop-in event is intended to empower business owners with the tools and knowledge necessary to succeed.

Participants can expect to receive guidance on various aspects of business management, including marketing, finance, and operational efficiency. The event is open to all businesses, regardless of size or sector, making it an inclusive opportunity for the local business community.

The drop-in event will take place on , at . Business owners are encouraged to attend and take advantage of the free support available. No prior registration is required, allowing for flexible participation.

For more information about the Rochdale Business Drop-In and other support services, visit the GM Business Growth Hub website or contact them directly. This initiative represents a significant opportunity for local businesses to access expert advice and foster growth in the Rochdale area.

Update timeline

  • 11 July 2026, 06:53: Business Clinic Drop-In Event Scheduled in Rochdale
  • 9 July 2026, 09:52: Free Business Support Available at Rochdale Drop-In Event

articles.title: Rochdale AFC Announces New Signings Ahead of Upcoming Season
articles.excerpt: The future of the Seven Sisters tower blocks in Rochdale is in question as tenant evictions continue and discussions about redevelopment options are revisited by Rochdale Boroughwide Housing.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 15 July 2026 at 09:01. 18 source updates have been combined into this article.

The future of the Seven Sisters tower blocks in Rochdale is in question as tenant evictions continue and discussions about redevelopment options are revisited by Rochdale Boroughwide Housing.

Latest update

Rochdale AFC has announced the appointment of a new manager following the departure of the previous head coach. The club confirmed the news on its official social media channels earlier today.

The identity of the new manager has not yet been disclosed. The club aims to improve its performance in the upcoming season after facing challenges in recent months.

Fans and club officials have expressed optimism about the new appointment, highlighting the need for strong leadership.

Further details regarding the manager's first match in charge and plans for the squad are expected to be released in the coming days.

Earlier developments

The future of the Seven Sisters tower blocks in Rochdale is in question as tenant evictions continue and discussions about redevelopment options are revisited by Rochdale Boroughwide Housing.

Hopwood Hall College has announced an extensive programme of events designed to enrich the educational experience for students while fostering engagement with the local community. The college's schedule includes ceremonies, community fun days, art exhibitions, and various educational initiatives, reflecting its commitment to providing diverse opportunities for both students and residents.

Among the key highlights, the college will host ceremonies to recognise and celebrate student achievements. These events will showcase the dedication and hard work of learners, offering them a platform to share their successes with family, friends, and the wider community. Such celebrations not only reinforce the importance of education but also promote personal growth and a sense of accomplishment among students.

In addition to academic recognitions, Hopwood Hall College will organise community fun days aimed at engaging local residents and families. These events will feature a variety of activities and entertainment suitable for all ages, fostering a spirit of togetherness and community pride. By inviting families to participate, the college seeks to strengthen its ties with the neighbourhood and create a welcoming environment for all.

Art exhibitions will also play a significant role in the college's events calendar, providing students with the opportunity to showcase their creative talents. These exhibitions will not only highlight the artistic skills of students but will also invite the community to appreciate and support local talent, thereby enhancing cultural engagement within the area.

For those interested in attending or participating in these events, further details are available on the college's official website. The events are open to the public, and the college encourages community members to join in the festivities and support its students. This initiative underscores Hopwood Hall College's role as a vital educational institution, dedicated to fostering a sense of belonging and collaboration within the community. As these events unfold, they are likely to enhance local community spirit and provide valuable opportunities for families and individuals to connect with the college and each other.

Rochdale AFC has officially unveiled its plans for the 2026 season, with a clear focus on enhancing player development and fostering community engagement. In a statement released on 9 July 2026, the club's management detailed a series of initiatives aimed at strengthening ties with local supporters while simultaneously improving the team's performance on the pitch.

Central to the club's strategy is an expanded youth academy programme, which is expected to provide increased opportunities for local talent to progress to the first team. This commitment to nurturing homegrown players reflects Rochdale AFC's dedication to developing talent from within the community, potentially leading to a more competitive squad and a stronger connection with local fans.

Update timeline

  • 15 July 2026, 09:01: Rochdale AFC Appoints New Manager Ahead of Upcoming Season
  • 11 July 2026, 15:30: Rochdale AFC Announces New Signings Ahead of Upcoming Season
